title: Keepfall Firebase Remote Config Contract (Phase 1)
description: JSON Schema for every live-tunable Keepfall key. Every key tunes a VALUE (a rate, a cap, a price, a cadence, a threshold) — never unlocks paywalled power, never grants a unit, never grants a tile, never adds a currency. All defaults trace to docs/00-source-of-truth.md. Firebase Remote Config keys are flat dot.case strings; the Unity RemoteConfig accessor mirrors these names verbatim. Min/max bounds exist to make every key safe to push live: an operator cannot, within these bounds, turn a value-tuner into a power-seller.

_meta.description: Contract metadata. Not a tunable; carried so a fetched config can be identified and audited.

schemaVersion.description: Version of THIS schema/contract shape. Bump when keys are added, removed, or re-bounded so clients can detect a contract they do not understand.

lastUpdated.description: Human note for when these defaults were last edited. Placeholder string only — never fabricate a timestamp; the build/seed tooling stamps the real value.

tuningPolicy.description: The one rule this whole file obeys.
tuningPolicy.const: tuning policy: values only, never power/units

tile.yield.t1.description: Stone earned per hour by a T1 (early) tile. SoT s2. Safe to tune: changes earn PACE only — Stone is the earned currency and buys no paywalled power. Tiles still come only from winning combat.

tile.yield.t2.description: Stone per hour for a T2 (mid) tile. SoT s2. Tunes the mid-game Stone curve (SoT s2 F2P daily curve) without altering what Stone can buy.

tile.yield.t3.description: Stone per hour for a T3 (late Phase 1) tile. SoT s2. Tunes late-game pace toward the 35–45 day F2P roster-completion target.

tile.cap.t1.description: Max Stone a T1 tile holds before a claim is required. SoT s2 (= yield x fillHours = 10 x 12). Tuning the cap changes how often a player must return to claim — a retention/convenience dial, not a power dial.

tile.cap.t2.description: Max Stone a T2 tile holds before claim. SoT s2 (= 25 x 12). Same idle-storage dial as t1.

tile.cap.t3.description: Max Stone a T3 tile holds before claim. SoT s2 (= 60 x 12). Same idle-storage dial as t1.

tile.fillHours.description: Hours for an empty tile to accrue to its cap. SoT s2 (all ranks fill in 12h). Accrual is real-time and survives app close, clamped to this window. Tunes return cadence only.

accelerator.price.t1.description: Shard price to instantly fill a T1 tile to its CURRENT cap. SoT s6 Product 1. Sells time the player already earned (one cap fill), never extra Stone beyond the cap. Price is a value dial; the per-purchase yield ceiling is enforced by accelerator.maxDaysPerPurchase, not by price.

accelerator.price.t2.description: Shard price to fill a T2 tile to current cap. SoT s6 (cheaper per Stone at higher tiers). Value dial only.

accelerator.price.t3.description: Shard price to fill a T3 tile to current cap. SoT s6. Value dial only.

accelerator.maxDaysPerPurchase.description: Hard cap: most days of yield a single accelerator purchase may grant. SoT s6 (<= 1 day of yield per purchase; never more than one full cap fill). Max is pinned at 1 so live tuning can only TIGHTEN this guardrail, never loosen it into selling outcomes.

accelerator.maxQueuedDays.description: Hard cap: most days of yield that may be queued on a single tile via acceleration. SoT s6 (no stacking past 3 days). Max pinned at 3 so tuning can only tighten the anti-stacking guardrail.

accelerator.minFillPctToOffer.description: A tile must be at least this fraction full before the accelerate option appears. SoT s6 (tile >= 30% filled). Minimum pinned at 0.30 so tuning can only make the offer RARER (raise the threshold), never push it earlier than the contract allows.

accelerator.lockedFirstMinutesD1.description: Minutes of D1 play during which the accelerator is fully locked. SoT s6 (locked during first 15 minutes of D1) and SoT s8 (D1 = pure play, no monetization). Minimum pinned at 15 so tuning can only EXTEND the no-sell window, never shorten it.

plus.priceUsd.description: Monthly price of Keepfall Plus (ONE tier only). SoT s6 Product 2. Price dial only — there is exactly one subscription tier; multi-tier subscriptions are a banned anti-pattern (SoT s10.8). Actual StoreKit price tier is set in App Store Connect; this value drives display copy and must match.

plus.yieldBonusPct.description: Tile-yield bonus for active Plus subscribers, as a fraction. SoT s6 (+50% on all owned tiles). This compresses TIME (roster completion ~22 vs ~40 days), it does not grant power, units, or tiles. Max pinned at 0.50 so tuning cannot inflate a convenience perk into a pay-to-win gap.

plus.extraDeckSlots.description: Extra saved-deck loadout slots granted by Plus. SoT s5/s6 (+1 deck slot, 4 total). A loadout-convenience perk, not a combat advantage. Max pinned at 1 to match the contract; deck.slots.plus carries the resulting absolute total.

plus.dailyQuestShardMultiplier.description: Multiplier on daily-quest Shard rewards for Plus. SoT s6 (2x daily quest Shard, +100 Shards/week). Adjusts Shard earn pace for subscribers; Shards buy time/cosmetics/retries, never paywalled power. Max pinned at 2.

plus.dailyLoginShardBonus.description: Extra Shards from the daily login bonus for Plus. SoT s6 (+5 Shards/day, +150/month). Earn-pace dial only. Max pinned at 5 to match the contract.

plus.freeTierSkipsPerWeek.description: Free Battle Pass tier-skips granted to Plus per week. SoT s6 (1 free tier skip/week). A completionist convenience; the Battle Pass is cosmetic-only on both tracks (SoT s7) so a skip never grants power. Max pinned at 1.

plus.trial.enabled.description: Whether the Plus free trial is offered. SoT s6 (7-day free trial, gated behind a remote-config flag). A live kill-switch for the trial; toggling it sells no power either way.

plus.trial.days.description: Length of the Plus free trial in days. SoT s6 (7-day free trial). Trial-length dial only; must align with the StoreKit introductory offer configured in App Store Connect.

deck.size.description: Cards in a deck (a 5-card hand draws from this). SoT s4/s5 (exactly 8 cards). A core rule, not a tunable balance lever — pinned at 8 so live config cannot break the validator. Exposed for parity/override safety only.

deck.avgElixirMin.description: Lower bound of the target average-elixir window the deck validator enforces. SoT s5 (target avg 2.6–3.0). A deck-construction guideline; tuning the window shapes meta variety, not unit power. Must stay <= deck.avgElixirMax.

deck.avgElixirMax.description: Upper bound of the target average-elixir window. SoT s5 (target avg 2.6–3.0). Must stay >= deck.avgElixirMin and <= elixir.cap.

deck.slots.f2p.description: Saved-deck loadout slots for a free player. SoT s5 (3 F2P). Loadout convenience; more slots never add combat power. Should stay <= deck.slots.plus <= deck.slots.maxPurchased.

deck.slots.plus.description: Loadout slots for a Plus subscriber (absolute total). SoT s5/s6 (4 Plus = 3 F2P + plus.extraDeckSlots). Convenience only.

deck.slots.maxPurchased.description: Ceiling on loadout slots reachable via Stone-purchased expansion. SoT s5 (up to 6 purchased). Expansion is bought with EARNED Stone and grants storage convenience, never power. Max pinned at 6.

elixir.regenPerSec.description: Elixir regenerated per second in a match. SoT s4 (1 per second). Tunes match pace symmetrically for player and AI; not a per-player advantage. A core feel lever exposed for global balancing only.

elixir.cap.description: Maximum stored elixir in a match. SoT s4 (cap 10). Symmetric match rule; max pinned at 10 to match the contract. Not a per-player power dial.

ai.threshold.apprentice.description: Minimum number of UNLOCKED UNITS (roster size, 0–24) at which the AI plays at Tier 1 Apprentice. SoT s4: difficulty advances on ROSTER EXPANSION, never raw days played; the D1–D3 etc. ranges are expected correlation, not the trigger. Starting tier, so 0. Tuning these thresholds re-shapes the difficulty curve against the SoT loss-rate targets (<=25% Apprentice, ~40% Tactician, ~55% Marshal) without a rebuild. Thresholds must be strictly ascending: apprentice < adept < tactician < commander < marshal.

ai.threshold.adept.description: Unlocked-unit count at which the AI advances to Tier 2 Adept. SoT s4 (roster-expansion trigger). Default 8 ~ end of the starter+early-core band. Must be > apprentice and < tactician.

ai.threshold.tactician.description: Unlocked-unit count at which the AI advances to Tier 3 Tactician. SoT s4. Default 12 ~ core roster filling in (SoT s2 Day 15–30 has 9–12 tiles owned / specialist tier in reach). Must be > adept and < commander.

ai.threshold.commander.description: Unlocked-unit count at which the AI advances to Tier 4 Commander (reads player decks). SoT s4. Default 18 ~ specialists landing, master tier in sight. Must be > tactician and < marshal.

ai.threshold.marshal.description: Unlocked-unit count at which the AI reaches Tier 5 Marshal (near-optimal). SoT s4. Default 22 ~ near roster completion (24 total = 6+10+6+2). Must be > commander.

retry.cost.single.description: Shard cost of a single PvE retry token. SoT s6 Product 3 (20 Shards each). A retry restores one attempt with IDENTICAL AI, map seed, and starting hand — the player must still win. The server (Cloudflare Worker) is the authority: cannot retry a win, cannot retry a retry, rewards capped at the first attempt. This key only prices the token; it never relaxes those server rules.

retry.cost.fivePack.description: Shard cost of a 5-pack of retry tokens. SoT s6 (90 Shards for 5). Price dial only; same server-authoritative retry rules apply.

retry.dailyCap.f2p.description: Daily cap on free-login retry tokens for an F2P player. SoT s6 (daily login 1/day, cap 3). Caps a free drip; does not change retry rules. The Worker enforces the cap.

retry.dailyCap.plus.description: Daily cap on login retry tokens for a Plus subscriber. SoT s6 (Plus +1/day, cap 5). Convenience pacing for subscribers; same server-authoritative retry rules. Should stay >= retry.dailyCap.f2p.

funnel.plus.maxReveals.description: Max times the Keepfall Plus reveal may be shown within a 30-day window. SoT s8 frequency caps (Plus shown max 3 times / 30 days). Max pinned at 3 so live tuning can only REDUCE pressure, never exceed the contract. The trigger engine reads player state, never wall-clock alone.

funnel.plus.windowDays.description: Rolling window (in days) over which funnel.plus.maxReveals applies — the 'max 3 Plus reveals' cap window. SoT s8 (Plus shown max 3 times / 30 days). Cadence dial only; pairs with funnel.plus.maxReveals and never sells power.

funnel.plus.postD30PerMonth.description: Plus reveals allowed per month for a non-converter after D30. SoT s8 (after D30, once per month). After D30 a non-converter sees no NEW triggers — focus shifts to retention; this is the single allowed recurring touch. Max pinned at 1.

funnel.accelHint.maxPerTilePerWeek.description: Max accelerator hints per tile per week. SoT s8 (max 1 per tile per week). Max pinned at 1 so tuning can only quiet the hint. A hint is a passive UI cue, never a modal or push (SoT s10).
funnel.accelHint.maxPerTilePerWeek.comment: Pairs with funnel.accelHint.cooldownDays: never hint a player who used an accelerator in the past 7 days.

funnel.accelHint.cooldownDays.description: Days after a player USES an accelerator during which accelerator hints are suppressed for them. SoT s8 (never to a player who used one in the past 7 days). Minimum pinned at 7 so tuning can only lengthen the quiet period, never shorten it below the contract.

funnel.retry.minConsecutiveLosses.description: Consecutive losses ON THE SAME MATCH required before a retry-token offer may appear. SoT s8 (only after 3 consecutive losses on the same match; never on first loss). Minimum pinned at 3 so tuning can only make the offer LATER/rarer, never surface it on the first loss.

funnel.postD30.suppressNewTriggers.description: Hard kill-switch for the post-D30 no-new-triggers branch. SoT s8 (after D30 a non-converter sees NO new triggers — focus shifts to retention). When true the funnel engine hard-branches off all new triggers for a non-converter at day_index >= 30. A safety toggle that sells no power either way.

funnel.d3.starterPackUsd.description: USD price of the D3 Shard starter pack (single banner in the Shop tab). SoT s7/s8 (D3 first offer: $0.99 Shard starter pack). Shard packs are always visible in Shop and never auto-presented after D3 (SoT s8). Price-display dial; actual StoreKit price tier is set in App Store Connect and must match. Min pinned at 0.99 to honor the stated entry price.

battlepass.seasonDays.description: Length of a Battle Pass season in days. SoT s7 (30-day season). Rewards are cosmetic-only on both free and premium tracks and the free track is F2P-completable; tuning season length never bundles power. Cadence dial only.

shop.rotationDays.description: Cosmetic-shop rotation period in days. SoT s7 (14-day rotation). Cadence dial for cosmetic freshness only.

shop.skusPerRotation.min.description: Minimum cosmetic SKUs offered per rotation. SoT s7 (3–5 SKUs per rotation). Content-volume dial; cosmetics only, never power. Must stay <= shop.skusPerRotation.max.

shop.skusPerRotation.max.description: Maximum cosmetic SKUs offered per rotation. SoT s7 (3–5 SKUs). Max pinned at 5 to match the contract. Must stay >= shop.skusPerRotation.min.
